summary: A single-story rectangular modern box building with a raised wooden floor interior, stone/stonebrick lower walls with repeating window bays, a white band mid-height, a wooden plank band near the top, and a shallow stone slab parapet around a flat roof. A white low outer curb surrounds the building on a dirt site.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular prism with a flat roof and parapet

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: white mid band and outer curb (could be smooth quartz or white concrete)

elements: row of small rectangular windows evenly spaced along each visible side
continuous white horizontal band above stone lower wall
wooden plank band above white band and wooden floor interior
stone slab parapet/edge around flat roof
outer low white curb surrounding building on ground level
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular foundation depressed into dirt; place a single-block-high outer curb of white blocks (quartz or concrete) inset one block from the building edge.

Construct the main walls: one or two courses of wooden plank at base and near top, stonebrick (or similar) row for lower wall with repeating 1x1 window openings separated by full stonebrick blocks, then a continuous white block band above the windows, then a wooden plank band under the stone slab parapet.
Place glass panes in each window bay (windows are 1 block tall and several bays across the longer sides).
Top the structure with a flat roof set flush with the parapet; use stone slabs for the parapet edge and fill interior roof with wooden planks at roof level as shown.
Corner vertical accents use cobblestone-like blocks (visible at one corner); replicate on all corners for consistency.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the white band and curb (quartz blocks vs white concrete) are uncertain based only on texture.
Lower wall block may be stonebrick or another similar dark stone; I labeled it stonebrick with moderate confidence.
Corner trim appears cobblestone but could be a different stone variant; confidence is moderate.
Precise interior layout, door location and any interior partitions are not visible except for raised wooden floor.
Exact external footprint dimensions in blocks are estimated from images (width/depth possibly off by a few blocks).
